panels.title: Incident Overview
panels.description: First-screen incident view: health, alerts, market context, user impact and SLOs.

panels.title: Overall Health
panels.description: Single-pane triage signal. HEALTHY = feed, workers and overlay fresh; IDLE = market closed before the first bar; STARTING = daemon still waiting on feed/workers/overlay; UNKNOWN = status metric missing.

panels.title: Active Alerts
panels.description: Currently firing Grafana alerts for live overlay, collector, and dependent services.

panels.title: Incident Triage Guide
panels.description: Suggested first checks when Overall Health is not HEALTHY.

options.content:
**Incident triage**

1. Check **Active Alerts** first.
2. If **Feed Healthy** is red: check Databento credentials, feed reconnects, and Railway logs.
3. If **Workers Healthy** is red: open Worker Liveness and restart the daemon if a worker is dead.
4. If **Overlay Fresh** is red: check Compute Cycle Errors and Overlay & Bar Age.
5. If traffic is missing while **Market Status** is open: check TradingView rollout, auth denied, and request rate.
6. If dashboards show no data: check Collector / Scrape Targets.

Runbook: [README](https://github.com/skippALGO/skipp-algo/blob/main/services/live_overlay_daemon/README.md)
7. If **Signal Pipeline Ready** is red: check **Open-Prep Snapshot**, **Watchlist Symbols**, **Producer Poll Age**, then Collector / Scrape Targets and signals-producer Railway logs.

---
**Quick links** · [Railway logs](https://railway.com/project/0616a3b7-7b7f-41d1-8fac-a0b8922c94ca/service/705582c5-ba8b-4c6e-848c-33bffe0a61b0/logs?environmentId=470fbd0f-894d-46cd-8722-6b072d255d99) · [Railway deployments](https://railway.com/project/0616a3b7-7b7f-41d1-8fac-a0b8922c94ca/service/705582c5-ba8b-4c6e-848c-33bffe0a61b0/deployments?environmentId=470fbd0f-894d-46cd-8722-6b072d255d99) · [GitHub Actions](https://github.com/skippALGO/skipp-algo/actions) · [Runbook: live-overlay on-call](https://github.com/skippALGO/skipp-algo/blob/main/services/live_overlay_daemon/OPS.md)

panels.title: Feed Healthy
panels.description: Live feed connection health (0 = unhealthy, 1 = healthy).

panels.title: Overlay Fresh
panels.description: Is the latest overlay calculation recent enough to trade on? Green means the last computed overlay is within the expected freshness window.

panels.title: Workers Healthy
panels.description: Background thread liveness (1 = all alive, 0 = at least one dead).

panels.title: External Checks
panels.description: External bridge scrape health (UptimeRobot + GitHub Workflows).

panels.title: Market Status
panels.description: Major session state (US regular or Europe regular); 1 = open, 0 = closed.

panels.title: Last Bar Age
panels.description: Age of the newest bar received from Databento.

panels.title: Service Status
panels.description: Daemon lifecycle state: UNKNOWN, STARTING, IDLE or OK. Combines feed readiness, worker liveness, overlay freshness and market-open context.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Daemon process uptime since the last start/restart.

panels.title: Overlay Symbols
panels.description: Number of distinct symbols currently held in the in-memory SMC overlay cache.

panels.title: Bar Symbols
panels.description: Number of distinct symbols with at least one 1-minute bar in the ingest cache.

panels.title: Bar Count
panels.description: Total 1-minute bars currently retained across all symbols.

panels.title: Process Resident Memory
panels.description: Daemon process resident memory as reported by the built-in Prometheus collector.

panels.title: Signal Pipeline Ready
panels.description: 1 = healthy: watchlist loaded, Open-Prep snapshot present, and a poll succeeded within the last 5 minutes. Age=0 is treated as not-ready. 0 = pipeline not ready.

targets.expr: (   (signals_producer_watchlist_symbols{job=~"signals_producer"} > bool 0) * (signals_producer_open_prep_snapshot_loaded{job=~"signals_producer"} > bool 0) * ((signals_producer_last_poll_age_seconds{job=~"signals_producer"} or on() vector(999999)) < bool 300) ) or on() vector(0)

panels.title: Open-Prep Snapshot
panels.description: 1 = Open-Prep snapshot loaded; 0 = missing.

panels.title: Watchlist Symbols
panels.description: Number of symbols loaded into the realtime signal watchlist.

panels.title: Producer Poll Age
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful realtime signal poll completed (age=0 before first poll).

panels.title: Global Market Sessions
panels.description: Regular trading session state for US, Europe, and Asia markets.

panels.title: Success Rate (%)
panels.description: Percentage of recent /smc_live HTTP requests that completed successfully. Displays NO TRAFFIC when no requests are observed.

panels.title: Market Traffic Health

targets.expr: (live_overlay_market_us_open{job=~"$job"} or vector(0)) + ((live_overlay_market_us_open{job=~"$job"} or vector(0)) * (((rate(live_overlay_smc_live_requests_total{job=~"$job"}[5m]) or vector(0)) > bool 0.001)))

panels.description: Synthetic signal that amplifies request health while the US regular trading session is open and suppresses it when closed.

panels.title: Market Data Freshness
panels.description: Are market-open bars still arriving on time? Shows freshness SLO over the last hour.

panels.title: Core Metrics Present
panels.description: Can Grafana still see the service? This panel turns red when the core daemon metric series disappear, which usually means the process is down or the scrape target is unreachable.

panels.title: Latency vs. SLO (ms)
panels.description: 5m rolling p95/p99 latency computed with histogram_quantile() over live_overlay_smc_live_latency_ms_bucket, plus fixed 500 ms SLO threshold.

panels.title: Error Budget Burn Rate
panels.description: Rate of bad events over 5m and 1h windows used for multi-window SLO alerting.

panels.title: Traffic Alert Armed
panels.description: Is production first-zero traffic alerting armed? Shows LIVE_OVERLAY_EXPECT_MARKET_TRAFFIC; 1 means ARMED and 0 means NOT ARMED.

panels.title: Operational Drill-down
panels.description: Immediate cause-finding panels after the top-level incident signals.

panels.title: Request Rate
panels.description: HTTP request rate to the daemon endpoints as observed by Grafana Alloy.

panels.title: Overlay & Bar Age
panels.description: Time since the overlay cache and the newest ingested bar were last refreshed.

panels.title: Compute Cycle Errors
panels.description: Rate of errors raised during SMC overlay compute cycles (full + flow refresh).

panels.title: Feed Health Counters
panels.description: Feed-level counters: reconnects, Databento errors, unexpected errors, circuit breakers and partial restarts.

panels.title: Worker Liveness
panels.description: Per-worker thread liveness: live_feed, overlay_refresh, flow_refresh, ingest_processor.

panels.title: Feed Circuit Breakers (Total)
panels.description: Cumulative number of times the feed circuit breaker opened since process start.

panels.title: Feed Partial Restarts (Total)
panels.description: Cumulative number of partial feed restarts (lighter than a full reconnect).

panels.title: Daemon Restarts (24h)
panels.description: Estimated number of daemon restarts in the last 24 hours based on uptime resets.

panels.title: Failure Mix
panels.description: Breakdown of compute/feed failures by category over time.

panels.title: Stale Budget Consumed (%)
panels.description: How much of the configured max_stale budget is currently consumed. 100 % means the overlay is at the stale threshold. This is derived from overlay_age_seconds / max_stale_seconds.

panels.title: Readiness Components Timeline
panels.description: Readiness components over time: feed healthy, workers healthy, overlay fresh, market open.

panels.title: Stale Responses (served)
panels.description: Count of health/status responses served while the overlay or feed data was stale.

panels.title: External Integrations
panels.description: Row grouping for external bridge health: UptimeRobot monitors and GitHub Actions workflows.

panels.title: Bridge Scrape Health Timeline
panels.description: External bridge scrape success over time (UptimeRobot + GitHub Workflows).

panels.title: GitHub Workflows — Latest Run Detail
panels.description: Per-workflow detail of the most recent run the daemon observed: trigger event (schedule / push / …), current status, run duration and age. One row per named workflow. Driven by the labelled live_overlay_github_workflow_* gauges (phase_code, latest_duration_seconds, latest_age_seconds, latest_success).

panels.title: UptimeRobot Monitor States
panels.description: Per-monitor UptimeRobot state over time. paused = monitor intentionally paused; unknown = unrecognized status code.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Bridge

targets.expr: (max by (job) (live_overlay_uptimerobot_bridge_enabled{job=~"$job"}) + on(job) max by (job) (live_overlay_uptimerobot_scrape_success{job=~"$job"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Whether the UptimeRobot API bridge is enabled and its last scrape succeeded.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Bridge Error
panels.description: Bridge error flag across the selected jobs/instances (0=OK, 1=ERROR).

panels.title: UptimeRobot Snapshot Age
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful UptimeRobot bridge poll.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Monitors
panels.description: Per-monitor up/down status and response time from UptimeRobot.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Bridge

targets.expr: (max by (job) (live_overlay_github_workflow_bridge_enabled{job=~"$job"}) + on(job) max by (job) (live_overlay_github_workflow_scrape_success{job=~"$job"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Whether the GitHub workflow bridge is enabled and its last scrape succeeded.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Bridge Error
panels.description: Bridge error flag across the selected jobs/instances (0=OK, 1=ERROR).

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Snapshot Age
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful GitHub workflow bridge poll.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Runs
panels.description: Snapshot counts of GitHub workflow runs by phase from the latest bridge scrape.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Status — Timeline
panels.description: Colour-coded latest status of each GitHub Actions workflow over time. Every row is one named workflow ({{workflow}} legend); the cell colour encodes the run conclusion — green=success, red=failure/timed-out/startup-failure, orange=cancelled/action-required, blue=in-progress/neutral, yellow=queued/stale, gray=unknown, purple=skipped. Series come from live_overlay_github_workflow_phase_code{workflow,event,workflow_id}, sampled at the daemon's GitHub workflow bridge interval (so runs shorter than one scrape interval may not appear).

panels.title: Reliability Drill-down
panels.description: Detailed restart, load, hotspot and backpressure diagnostics after the top-level SLO and impact signals.

panels.title: Restart Causes (24h)
panels.description: Restart cause distribution over the last 24 hours, extracted from per-cause counters.

panels.title: Hotspots — Symbols (Top)
panels.description: Most requested SMC overlay symbols by request count.

panels.title: Hotspots — Timeframes (Top)
panels.description: Most requested SMC timeframes by request count.

panels.title: Ingest Queue Backpressure
panels.description: Current ingest queue depth versus its configured maximum (left axis) and the rate of dropped messages (right axis).

panels.title: Ingest Queue Lag (ms)
panels.description: End-to-end latency from bar arrival to ingestion completion.

panels.title: Provider Health
panels.description: Service-owner detail: live news provider health and provider-level degradation context.

panels.title: News Provider State Codes
panels.description: Legend for News Provider panels directly below.

options.content: **News provider state codes:** `0` = unknown / no data, `1` = degraded, `2` = ok, `3` = disabled (not ingested). The **News Providers — Ingest Status & Reason** table shows the human-readable reason (e.g. API key missing, no active subscription, provider disabled) and whether each provider is actually consumed.

panels.title: News Providers — State Code
panels.description: Per-provider news state code: 0=unknown, 1=degraded, 2=ok, 3=disabled (not ingested).

panels.title: News Provider Health State
panels.description: Aggregate news provider health flags (ok / degraded / unknown).

panels.title: News Snapshot Age
panels.description: Age of the smc_live_news_snapshot.json provider state.

panels.title: News Provider Counts
panels.description: Aggregate provider counts (ok/degraded/unknown/disabled/consumed).

panels.title: News Providers — OK/Degraded (Drill-down)
panels.description: Per-provider ok/degraded/unknown state codes over time.

panels.title: News Providers — Ingest Status & Reason
panels.description: Per-provider ingest status with the human-readable degraded reason (API key missing, no active subscription, provider disabled, …) and whether the provider is actually consumed.

panels.title: Collector / Scrape Targets
panels.description: Service-owner detail: telemetry pipeline health, scrape target liveness, and collector memory.

panels.title: Scrape Targets Up
panels.description: Scrape target liveness from Alloy for alloy, signals_producer and live_overlay jobs.

panels.title: Collector Resident Memory
panels.description: Resident memory for Alloy, signals_producer and live_overlay jobs. live_overlay and signals_producer expose prefixed metric names.

panels.title: TradingView Credential Age
panels.description: Hours since the TradingView Playwright storage-state login (meta.authValidatedAt) was last validated, from the daily credential-health report (tv_storage_state_age probe). Policy TTL is 72h: green < 57.6h (80% warn band), yellow 57.6–72h, red once the cached login is older than 72h and must be re-captured. N/A when no report/age is available.

panels.title: TradingView Credential Status
panels.description: Whether the latest credential-health report considers the TradingView storage-state login valid (severity not error). 1 = valid (cached login still within the 72h TTL), 0 = expired/error → re-capture TV_STORAGE_STATE. N/A when no report has been ingested yet.

panels.title: Railway Resources
panels.description: Service-owner detail: Railway container capacity and bridge health for production operations.

panels.title: Railway Metrics Bridge
panels.description: Whether the Railway metrics bridge is enabled and its last poll succeeded.

targets.expr:
(
  max by (job) (live_overlay_bridge_enabled{job=~"$job",bridge="railway_metrics"})
  +
  max by (job) (live_overlay_bridge_scrape_success{job=~"$job",bridge="railway_metrics"})
)
or on(job) label_replace(vector(0), "job", "live_overlay", "", "")

panels.title: Railway Metrics Snapshot Age
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful Railway metrics poll.

panels.title: Railway Metrics Error
panels.description: Latest Railway metrics bridge error class. OK when healthy.

panels.title: Railway CPU Cores
panels.description: CPU cores used per Railway service (from Railway GraphQL metrics).

panels.title: Railway Memory Used Ratio
panels.description: Memory usage / memory limit per Railway service.

panels.title: Railway Disk Usage (GB)
panels.description: Disk usage per Railway service in GB.

panels.title: Railway Network RX (GB)
panels.description: Network receive (RX) per Railway service in GB.

panels.title: Railway Network TX (GB)
panels.description: Network transmit (TX) per Railway service in GB.

panels.title: Railway Memory Limit (GB)
panels.description: Memory limit per Railway service in GB.

list.label: Prometheus job (advanced)
list.name: job
list.description: Prometheus job label for the live overlay daemon.

title: SMC Live Overlay API
description: Live-overlay daemon operational dashboard: feed health, worker liveness, SMC overlay freshness, external bridge status, provider health, daily experiment metrics, and Railway container resources.
